equivalence_argument: No complete mapping was found: current ExecutorRegistry has no TTL or interval-based cleanup method, only capacity eviction of the oldest inactive executor.

negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing cleanup behavior that removes expired inactive executors after a cleanup interval while retaining active executors older than TTL.
related_but_insufficient_code: ExecutorRegistry stores registration_time but exposes no TTL cleanup method for expired inactive executors.
ExecutorRegistry::clear removes all executors and is not an expired-inactive cleanup policy.
remove_oldest_inactive only runs when register_executor sees capacity pressure; it does not remove inactive records by age or explicitly preserve active old records under a cleanup interval.

rationale: The cleanup predicate explicitly keeps active executors and applies TTL only to inactive entries, which protects active executor availability from time-based eviction. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: ExecutorRegistry cleanup must remove expired inactive executors after the cleanup interval, but must retain active executors even when their registration timestamp is older than the configured TTL.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create a registry with a short TTL, insert one active and one inactive executor with old registration times, force last_cleanup to be older than 300 seconds or expose cleanup through a test helper, trigger registration/cleanup, and assert only the inactive expired executor is removed.
summary: Registry TTL cleanup preserving active executors is absent from the current port.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Create old active and inactive executor records and trigger the expected cleanup path; current registry has no cleanup API or TTL setting to exercise.
title: Registry cleanup preserves active executors
